title: Spill av knapp
description:
Legg til som favoritt.
Dele ikon
Spill av knapp
Video
10 Software Developers with real world impact  (Location: Stavanger or Copenhagen)
Laerdal Medical AS
Frist
15.4.2026
Ansettelsesform
Fast
One process, multiple teams, meaningful work!
Make an impact that matters
At Laerdal, technology is a means to a bigger end: helping save lives. Our software and digital solutions are used by healthcare professionals, students, and first responders worldwide, supporting Laerdal’s goal to help save one million more lives, every year, by 2030. Do you believe strong engineering thrives in an environment of trust, collaboration, and learning? Then you are the perfect candidate for us!
How we build software at Laerdal
You’ll work in cross functional product teams where engineers have a real voice in both product direction and technical decisions. We prioritize solid engineering practices enabling teams to ship with confidence, maintain high quality, and continuously improve.
We’re hiring for multiple teams right now/ We have several exciting projects coming up
With three inspiring, cutting egde tech-projects underway, Laerdal offers a range of opportunities where you can bring your strengths to life and help create solutions that truly matter:
Team #1: RQI (Resuscitation Quality Improvement)
Elevate CPR performance through real-time measurement, feedback, and continuous learning at scale. Work on software connecting devices, learning flows, and analytics used globally by clinical staff.
Locations: Stavanger, Norway or Copenhagen, Denmark
Team #2: Sim to Improve
Sim to Improve combines simulation, quality improvement methodology, and AI-driven insight to help hospitals uncover workflow challenges, test planned changes and implement safer systems of care.
You will help shape the platform’s architecture, performance, user experience, and scalability.
Location: Stavanger, Norway
Team #3: Simulated Instruments
Simulated Instruments develops the software behind realistic, instrument-like experiences used in medical training and simulation. You will build digital components that interact with physical devices, provide responsive realtime feedback, and integrate with larger simulation systems.
Location: Stavanger, Norway
What you’ll do
Create, build and evolve software from idea to production i
Make an impact on Laerdal’s architecture and design
Practice engineering hygiene: testing, code reviews, documentation, CI/CD
Contribute to improve quality, performance, and developer experience
Who you are
Curious and pragmatic. You value sustainable solutions over quick fixes, share knowledge freely, and contribute to an inclusive and highly skilled engineering environment.
Core profile (standardized across teams, ~70–80%)
Professional software development experience (5+ years)
Enthusiastic about modern development principles
Love to deliver production codes
Enjoy collaborating
Conscientious mindset to sustainability, risk and safety
Proficient in English (written and spoken)
Technologies you may work with
Backend: C# / .NET
Cloud: Azure (security, observability, operations)
Frontend: React
Mobile: React Native
APIs, integrations, databases, distributed systems
What we offer:
Interesting and meaningful work contributing to our mission
Opportunities for personal and professional growth
A great, diverse, inclusive, and international work environment
Innovative and forward-thinking technology
Competitive compensation and benefits package
Flexible work arrangements, an extensive wellbeing program with social, cultural and sports activities and benefits such as discounted public transport, wardrobe with towel service, company cabins, and more. (Norway specific).
Apply
Please send your CV and a brief motivation letter. In your application form:
Indicate which team(s) you’re most interested in (RQI, Sim to Improve, Simulated Instruments)
Indicate your preferred location (Stavanger or Copenhagen)
Provide your years of experience per technology (e.g., C#/.NET, Azure, React, Mobile, Databases)
Share any domain interests (education technology, clinical training, simulation hardware, data/AI)
We map roles to our career framework for clarity and fairness across teams. Our goal is a professional, fair and efficient experience, while giving you the freedom to explore the teams where you feel most at home and can use your strengths to the fullest.
For questions about the positions, please contact:
Mads Bak for RQI (mads.bak@laerdal.com)
Eva Skarstein for Sim to Improve (eva.skarstein@laerdal.com)
Michel Eid for Simulated Instruments (michel.eid@laerdal.com)
Ready to apply?
Please register your application by 15.04.2026.
We look forward to hearing from you!
Please note that we use Semac background checks in our recruitment process.(Norway specific).

Rapporter annonse

analysis.summary: Laerdal Medical is hiring 10 software developers across 3 product teams in Stavanger (Norway) and Copenhagen (Denmark). The teams build healthcare simulation and CPR training software used globally. The stack is C#/.NET backend, React/React Native frontend, and Azure cloud. The role is hands-on engineering in cross-functional product teams with strong engineering culture (CI/CD, code reviews, testing). The ad is written in English, the working language is explicitly listed as English, and the mission (saving lives through technology) is compelling. Deadline is April 15, 2026.
analysis.why_foreigner_friendly: Excellent fit for an English-speaking foreigner. The job ad itself is written in English, the Arbeidsspråk field explicitly states 'Engelsk', and the role targets an international candidate pool (locations in both Norway and Denmark). Laerdal Medical is a well-established global company (founded 1955, significant international operations) with the HR infrastructure to handle work permits. No Norwegian language requirement anywhere in the listing. The tech stack (C#, React, Azure) is entirely international. 10 open positions signals serious hiring intent rather than a token posting.
